translations.*- 本文僅為個人筆記夾雜些許個人觀點，在這資訊碎片化的年代，強烈建議要自行閱讀: *- This article is a personal note mixed with some personal views. In this age of fragmented information, it is highly recommended to read the original book yourself to systematically absorb its essence.*
translations.*- 筆記的意義是之後回頭來看，比較容易快速定位到想複習的點。*\n\n: *- The purpose of notes is to make it easier to quickly locate key points for review later.*
translations.*- 部分內容直接摘錄原文。*\n\n: *- Some content is directly quoted from the original text.*
translations.- Acquisition: How to Attract More Users:
- Acquisition: How to Attract More Users

- Activation: How to guide users to complete tasks that help them understand the product's value (e.g., Alarm App, onboarding users to set up an alarm)

- Retention: Increase return rate and frequency of use

- Referrer: Let Your Users and Content Bring More Traffic

- Revenue: Digitally assess the revenue generated by users
translations.- After the initial trial phase, the pro:
- After the initial trial phase, the product model tends to stabilize; at this point, it is more suitable to optimize throughput (e.g., given X resources, achieve Y output).

- Provide Predictability for Business Requirements (Same as Above)
translations.- Aligning Goals \n Align personal vis:
- Aligning Goals  
  Align personal vision with company goals. Not understanding the current company goals can lead to team dysfunction.

- Align Core Values  
  This refers to consensus and tacit understanding regarding ways of working and what is important. Team core values are not fixed and should evolve over time.

- Balance  
  Assign different visions, autonomy, and ownership according to team members' skills and growth; collaborate and grow together (e.g., newcomers expect to understand company workflows, veterans handle code reviews and mentoring); everyone should have opportunities for growth.

- The core values of the group outweigh those of individuals.  
  This may lead to some people leaving and requires patience and time to achieve; there are also many challenges (e.g., when someone leaves, the core values may be questioned).

- ## Sense of Achievement  
  Results should bring a sense of achievement. As a manager, you must not let your team members lose their enthusiasm.
translations.- Does this issue really need a manager:
- Does this issue really need a manager to decide?

- Have you asked your teammates for their advice?

- Is there anyone more experienced who can offer advice?

- Would you make the same decision if you reconsider now?
translations.- Don't let others (other teams) clean u:
- Don't let others (other teams) clean up the mess; your own (team's) mistakes must be owned by yourselves.

- Be Loyal and Respectful to All Team Members  
  Having core values provides clear criteria for recruitment or termination and sets a standard for how to get things done.
translations.- External Resources or Expectations fro:
- External Resources or Expectations from Above (Feedback/Tool)

- Delegate
translations.- Foster a positive engineering culture:
- Foster a positive engineering culture environment (e.g., Code Review, annual meetings…)

- Creating an attractive work environment

- Like managing a brand

- Team members working together

- Strengthen human connections (e.g., birthday celebrations)

- First, make team members feel proud of the team
translations.- How many technical issues does it caus:
- How many technical issues does it cause?

- Is it a personal issue?

- Is it an isolated issue with a specific member? (Is it simply that they don’t understand the goal?)

- Does this issue recur in other teams?
translations.- Internal teams regularly answer commun:
- Internal teams regularly answer community questions weekly (e.g., Stackoverflow) to increase exposure.

- Hiding recruitment Easter eggs in code (e.g., Web Developer Tools)

- Share with the community the problems our team encountered and the solutions we implemented (article or talk)

- Holding a hackathon

- Create side projects (e.g., open source projects)
translations.- Language: English\n\n- Author: Oren El:
- Language: English

- Author: Oren Ellenbogen

- Publication Year: 2013

- [Official Website](https://leadingsnowflakes.com/){:target="_blank"}

- Thanks to [Prime Minister Hai](https://twitter.com/tzangms){:target="_blank"} for the recommendation
translations.- Must write down the task details\n\n: - Must write down the task details
translations.- Premature Optimization Is a Disaster\n:
- Premature Optimization Is a Disaster

- Optimize current problems first; avoid optimizing just for the sake of optimization.

- Even if you are not responsible for the entire project, you can still optimize internal operations. Major successes often come from the accumulation of small improvements.

- As managers, we must demonstrate the motivation behind our decisions.

- Build a culture of business learning (value) rather than just building solutions (the focus is on the business problems we aim to solve).

- Optimization of Efficiency vs Optimization of Throughput:

  Optimization of Efficiency: Reducing the time to complete a single task  
  Optimization of Throughput: Increasing the number of tasks completed within a time frame (e.g., a quarter)

- Know the impact of each optimization.

- The Importance of Automation (Save Time Once and for All)
translations.- Provide a productive and happy work en:
- Provide a productive and happy work environment

- Understand the Why of a task, not just the How

- Be able to receive genuine feedback

- Opportunity to lead other members

- Able to share work results
translations.- Set Expectations According to Ability\:
- Set Expectations According to Ability

- Ability to train others to change

- Drive Change, Not Complaints
translations.- Simple Thank You Letter (Thank You for:
- Simple Thank You Letter (Thank You for Your Assistance)

- Rotating Team Work

- Internal tech annual meeting, sharing with each other

- Observe user behavior together and brainstorm optimization ideas collaboratively

- Invite a teammate from another team to join our work
translations.- Synchronous Communication: For example:
- Synchronous Communication: For example, in the product development process, design resources are needed; when entering the engineering development phase, do we already have clear specifications to proceed with development? Or are we still waiting? Is there anything we can do in the meantime?

- Infrastructure: Making Code Scalable and Maintainable

- Automation: Use automation to handle tedious manual tasks, saving time and reducing errors
translations.- Task Completed\n\n- Maintain a Passion:
- Task Completed

- Maintain a Passion for Learning

- Maintain enthusiasm for sharing and teaching

- Know the bottom line sense of getting things done
translations.- To do effective data tracking, you mus: - To do effective data tracking, you must understand the impact of your efforts
translations.- Understand transactional trust and rel:
- Understand transactional trust and relational trust.

- Transactional Trust — Whether people will keep their promises and complete tasks.

- Relational Trust — Do people act in ways that build and protect relationships?
translations.1. Compared to covering up, have I openl:
1. Compared to covering up, have I openly shared my failure experiences? (This can be writing articles or sending emails to everyone)

2. Forget to consolidate discussion results (Get into the habit of recording 1:1 and discussion outcomes)

3. Using the wrong feedback medium can prevent you from identifying the real issues (find the appropriate feedback channel based on team culture, e.g., 1:1).

4. Delayed Feedback  
   We need to recognize that engineers enjoy challenging themselves and improving their skills, while also wanting respect and feedback from their managers. Our role is to lead the team's growth, so we should never delay giving feedback when the opportunity arises. Not making a decision is also a decision. Moreover, once the culture of feedback weakens, it becomes much harder to reignite.
translations.1. Did I show empathy?\n\n2. Did I clear:
1. Did I show empathy?

2. Did I clearly state my expectations?

3. Do I lead by example?
translations.1. The team should have a vision, not ju:
1. The team should have a vision, not just focus on completing tasks.

2. Managers Need to Distinguish Between Needs and Wants

3. Optimize the team to complete the right things faster, rather than doing more things.

4. Establish Good Communication with Other Team Managers  
   The author suggests sharing your team's status, obstacles, challenges, upcoming key tasks, and the reasons behind them during bi-weekly manager meetings.

5. When there is a difference in priority opinions with other teams, explain by bringing up other factors (e.g., completing this will reduce customer complaints, provide a one-time permanent solution, or have a multiplier effect…).

6. First understand where external teams need our help and proactively follow up closely.

7. Next, outline the points where our team needs help from external teams.

8. Make a checklist to confirm that all items are discussed during the meeting; if not, follow up with relevant managers afterward to explore other possibilities.

9. If it is not possible, weigh the potential delay or alternative solutions, and inform stakeholders (to prevent gossip behind the scenes).

10. Everything Is a Trade-off
translations.1. To reduce the risk of deliverables, t:
1. To reduce the risk of deliverables, the author suggests breaking down requirements into many small iterative features and sharing this idea with other teams.

2. Scale and performance — always have a backup plan  
   Will this feature affect performance (or cause other issues)? Can we know in advance? Is there a backup plan (toggle)? If there is no backup plan, it’s better not to implement it, as it can impact team confidence.

3. Break down tasks into smaller tasks to reduce deadline risks  
   It may be difficult at first, but it can be trained and learned.

4. Leverage Peer Pressure  
   Break tasks into parts for teammates to collaborate on and work together (Code Review is also part of this).

5. Continuous Internal and External Communication  
   Internal: Ensure expectations, alignment, deadlines, and resources.  
   External: Communicate clearly, and if time is tight, skip non-essential meetings.

6. Support, Bug Fixing, Documentation  
   It's not enough to just release features; you also need to provide good customer support, fix bugs, and maintain documentation.

7. Conduct effective retrospectives and delegate tasks to provide leadership opportunities for others.

8. Select a few tasks to lead by example.

9. Ask teammates what they have learned, what motivates them to be more proactive, and what they dislike.
translations.1. 定義團隊願景\nEX: 作者的團隊是做爬蟲的，他的團隊願景就是「To bu:
1. Define the Team Vision  
EX: The author's team works on web crawlers, and their team vision is "To build the largest, most informative profile-database in the world."  
Note that this is a vision, not a short-term goal or something they want to avoid.
translations.2. 定義團隊核心價值\n在挑選核心價值時可以「這個價值重要到會因為沒有而開除某:
2. Define Core Team Values  
When choosing core values, ask yourself, "Is this value important enough to fire someone for not having it?"  
Write down the core values and the reasons behind them.  
The author provides the following core values he wrote:
translations.> \"If you can’t make engineering decisi: > "If you can’t make engineering decisions based on data, then make engineering decisions that result in data."
translations.> \"If you want to achieve anything in t: > "If you want to achieve anything in this world, you have to get used to the idea that not everyone will like you."
translations.> \"Imagine that someone from Team A dro: > "Imagine that someone from Team A drops a feature that Team B needs due to an urgent support issue. Without communicating this priority change to Team B, trust will decrease even if the priority change is justified."
translations.> \"There is no one right way, there are: > "There is no one right way, there are only tradeoffs."
translations.> \"You can’t empower people by approvin: > "You can’t empower people by approving their actions. You empower by designing the need for your approval out of the system."
translations.> *- Some content is directly quoted fro: > *- Some content is directly quoted from the original text.*

translations.> *- This article is a personal note mix: > *- This article is a personal note combined with some personal opinions. In this age of fragmented information, it is highly recommended to read the original book yourself to systematically grasp its core.*
translations.> If you want to achieve something, you: > If you want to achieve something, you must accept that not everyone will like your ideas.
translations.Acquisition：如何引入更多使用者\n: Acquisition: How to Attract More Users
translations.Activation：如何引導使用者完成讓他了解產品價值的任務（EX: 鬧鐘 A: Activation: How to guide users to complete tasks that help them understand the product's value (e.g., Alarm App, onboarding users to set up an alarm)
translations.Delegate 的部分，我們可以提供一頁的 Paper 闡述我們的期待、簡單的: For the Delegate part, we can provide a one-page paper outlining our expectations and simple examples.
translations.External 關於外部或上面期待的資源 (Feedback/Tool)\n: External Resources or Expectations from Above (Feedback/Tool)
translations.Must 寫下任務內容\n: Must write down the task details

translations.Revenue：數字化評估使用者帶來的收入\n: Revenue: Digitally assess the revenue generated by users
translations.Scale and performance — always have a ba:
Scale and performance — always have a backup plan  
Will this feature affect performance (or cause other issues)? Can we know in advance? Is there a backup plan (toggle)? If there is no backup plan, it’s better not to implement it, as it can impact team confidence.
translations.“Leading Snowflakes The Engineering Mana: “Leading Snowflakes The Engineering Manager Handbook” — Oren Ellenbogen
translations.「 imagine that someone from Team A drops: "Imagine that someone from Team A drops a feature that Team B needs due to an urgent support issue. Without communicating this priority change to Team B, trust will decrease even if the priority change is justified."
translations.「If you can’t make engineering decisions: "If you can’t make engineering decisions based on data, then make engineering decisions that result in data."
translations.「If you want to achieve anything in this: "If you want to achieve anything in this world, you have to get used to the idea that not everyone will like you."
translations.「There is no one right way, there are on: "There is no one right way, there are only tradeoffs."
translations.「You can’t empower people by approving t: "You can’t empower people by approving their actions. You empower by designing the need for your approval out of the system."
translations.一起觀察使用者使用狀況，一起腦力激盪提出優化方向\n: Observe user behavior together and brainstorm optimization ideas collaboratively
translations.不像程式，人有期待、需要、夢想要顧及。\n\n: Unlike programs, people have expectations, needs, and dreams to consider.
translations.不即時的 Feedback\n我們需要注意，工程師喜歡挑戰自我、提升技能，同時也:
Delayed Feedback  
We need to recognize that engineers enjoy challenging themselves and improving their skills, while also wanting respect and feedback from their managers. Our role is to lead the team's growth, so we should never delay giving feedback when the opportunity arises. Not making a decision is also a decision. Moreover, once the culture of feedback weakens, it becomes much harder to reignite.
translations.不要成為團隊的技術瓶頸，我們的任務是提升團隊能力、探索新技術、提升公司對外或隊內: Do not become the technical bottleneck of the team. Our mission is to enhance the team's capabilities, explore new technologies, and broaden the company's internal and external technical vision. Possible actions include researching technical issues in advance, sharing findings with teammates and letting them execute, resolving company technical debt, improving processes to increase development efficiency, adopting new technologies, open-sourcing company technology, providing open APIs, hosting external hackathons, and more.
translations.不要讓別人(其他團隊)來收拾善後，自己(團隊)的錯誤自己要承擔\n: Don't let others (other teams) clean up the mess; your own (team's) mistakes must be owned by yourselves.
translations.了解交易信任與關係信任。\n: Understand transactional trust and relational trust.
translations.交易信任 — — 人們是否會履行承諾並完成任務\n: Transactional Trust — Whether people will keep their promises and complete tasks
translations.以身作則是不錯的方法，時不時參與團隊的開發示範如何計畫、產出好的功能展現我們想傳: Leading by example is a good approach. Occasionally participate in the team's development to demonstrate how to plan and deliver quality features that reflect the values we want to convey. Also, focus more on explaining the Why? and less on the How?
translations.但也不能完全將自己從任務中抽離，完全與任務細節抽量導致與團隊成員斷開連結，對於執: However, you cannot completely detach yourself from tasks; fully distancing from task details can cause disconnection from team members, posing significant long-term risks to execution outcomes, priorities, and trust.
translations.作者使用以下表格進行記錄：\n\n: The author uses the following table for recording:
translations.作者在過去近 20 年的工作經歷中，從原本的軟體工程師一步步踏入管理職；擔任過不: The author has nearly 20 years of work experience, transitioning step by step from a software engineer to management roles; having served as a Technical Lead and Engineering Manager in both large companies and startups. This book details the challenges engineers face when moving into management and the methods to organize and solve them.
translations.作者建議可以從 15–20% 比例開始調配，本來是 100% as Maker，: The author suggests starting with a 15–20% allocation. Originally 100% as a Maker, it could now be 20% as Maker / 80% as Manager (depending on the team size and members' abilities, 50% / 50% is also possible). The key is not to spend 100% on engineering development but to invest more effort in management.
translations.作者建議建立兩個 Calendar ，一個是 as Maker (工程師)、一個: The author suggests creating two Calendars: one as a Maker (engineer) and one as a Manager. Each morning, spend 15–30 minutes organizing your thoughts and planning your day, including what tasks to do, meetings, and continuous free time slots that can be used to tackle tasks (as a Maker).
translations.作者建議，每個月回頭審查，可以與老闆或其他管理者或其他同事分享討論決策（至少要分: The author suggests conducting a monthly review, sharing and discussing decisions with your boss, other managers, or colleagues (sharing at least half of the issues). Listen to others' perspectives; anonymity can be used to protect individuals, focusing on the issue, not the person, and keep a record.
translations.作者想要打要一個快樂的工作環境、隊友之間了解任務的期待、新挑戰；而且要能持續保持: The author aims to create a happy work environment where teammates understand task expectations and face new challenges, while continuously maintaining their passion.
translations.作者所述，現在身為管理者，但我們仍需同時處理任務；可利用的空檔專注時間對我們來說: The author states that even as managers, we still need to handle tasks; the available focus time is more important to us now than before.
translations.作者提供的紀錄 Sheet 範本\n\n: Template of the Record Sheet Provided by the Author
translations.作者提到一開始很不習慣，因為本來是朋友的同事現在變成部署；他害怕會傷害本來的關係: The author mentioned that at first, it was hard to get used to, since colleagues who were once friends became subordinates. He was afraid of damaging the original relationship, so he took on all the finishing tasks himself. But eventually, he realized that the more he tried to protect, the farther he distanced himself from his teammates. By repeatedly burying himself in work without sharing, he caused his teammates to lose faith.
translations.作者提到極度透明的文化，讓團隊成員有完整的 Context 能提高推動決策的能力: The author mentions that an extremely transparent culture, where team members have full context, can enhance their ability to drive decisions.
translations.作者提到要跨出去尋找提升管理能力的，公開徵求 Feedback & 提高管理技能: The author mentions stepping out to seek ways to improve management skills by openly requesting feedback and enhancing managerial abilities; being a manager with the same passion as when working as an engineer.
translations.作者提到，可以在需要專心的時間透過一些動作傳達給隊友，暫時不要打擾我！\n\n: The author mentions that you can signal to your teammates during focused work time with certain actions to indicate, "Please don't disturb me for now!"
translations.作者最後提到，如果你管理的任務太多完全無法做工程 (as Maker)的事，與任: The author finally mentions that if you have too many management tasks and cannot do engineering (as a Maker), losing touch with tasks and technology, consider working from home a few days a week to isolate yourself from the company or join hackathons.
translations.作者闡述，組織為了能做更多事會拆分很多小組進行快速決策處理；對於各小組的方向定義: The author explains that organizations split into many small teams to enable faster decision-making and execution; defining each team's direction is actually easy (e.g., iOS team focuses on iOS apps), but aligning the goals of all teams is the difficult part.
translations.使用錯誤的 Feedback 媒介，沒得到真正的問題（依照團隊文化找到適合的 F: Using the wrong feedback medium can prevent you from identifying the real issues (find the appropriate feedback channel based on team culture, e.g., 1:1).
translations.保持分享、教學熱忱\n: Maintain enthusiasm for sharing and teaching
translations.做好回顧及委派任務，提供其他人領導機會\n: Conduct effective retrospectives and delegate tasks to provide leadership opportunities for others
translations.做為工程師時我們喜歡有連續不被打斷的時間讓我們保持在 Context 中去解決困: As engineers, we like to have uninterrupted time to stay in context and solve difficult problems; but as managers, we need to frequently step out to help the team and care for teammates, so being interrupted is actually part of a manager’s job.
translations.優化可能不會用到的程式意義不大。\n\n: Optimizing code that may never be used is not very meaningful.
translations.優化團隊更快的完成正確的事而不是完成更多的事\n: Optimize the team to complete the right things faster, rather than doing more things.
translations.優化效率 vs 優化吞吐量問題：\n優化效率：解決單一 task 的時間\n優化:
Optimization of Efficiency vs Optimization of Throughput:

Optimization of Efficiency: Reducing the time to complete a single task  
Optimization of Throughput: Increasing the number of tasks completed within a time frame (e.g., a quarter)
translations.優化當前問題為重，不要為了優化而優化\n: Optimize current problems first; avoid optimizing just for the sake of optimization
translations.先了解外部團隊需要我們幫忙的地方並且主動密切追蹤\n: First understand where external teams need our help and proactively follow up closely
translations.先讓成員是對團隊感到驕傲的\n: First, make team members feel proud of the team
translations.內部團隊每週定時對外回答社群問題 (EX: Stackoverflow..)，加: Internal teams regularly answer community questions weekly (e.g., Stackoverflow) to increase exposure.
translations.內部技術年會，互相分享\n: Internal tech annual meeting, sharing with each other
translations.再來提出我們團隊需要外部團隊幫忙的點\n: Next, outline the points where our team needs help from external teams.
translations.分享真實想法對雙方都很難，但這是身為管理者的責任。\n\n: Sharing honest thoughts is difficult for both sides, but it is the responsibility of a manager.
translations.分配以上各任務給團隊成員，大家一起為找到好人才貢獻一份心力。\n\n: Assign the above tasks to team members so everyone can contribute to finding great talent together.
translations.列好需要確認的清單，確保在會議上有討論到；如果沒有可以在會後拉相關的經理討論看有: Make a checklist to confirm that all items are discussed during the meeting; if not, follow up with relevant managers afterward to explore other possibilities.
translations.功能能提升 Retention -> 釋出功能 -> 學習 -> 調整＆重複。\: Features can improve retention -> Release features -> Learn -> Adjust & repeat.
translations.即使不是整個專案的負責人，我們仍可就內部運作進行優化，大的成功多半來自小部分的優: Even if you are not responsible for the entire project, you can still optimize internal operations. Major successes often come from the accumulation of small improvements.
translations.只是某個成員的獨立問題？（是不是單純只是他不了解目標？）\n: Is it an isolated issue with a specific member? (Is it simply that they don’t understand the goal?)
translations.可以先實驗或發布給最小受眾嗎？避免功能很大包結果最後沒人用。\n\n: Can you first experiment or release it to the smallest audience? This avoids having a large feature that ends up unused.
translations.可以投資 20% 時間減少 80% 的開發時間嗎？尤其是令人痛苦的點\n\n: Can you invest 20% of the time to reduce 80% of the development time? Especially the painful parts.
translations.可以花時間寫下激勵隊友的方法及詢問主管是否太保護隊友？\n\n: You can take time to write down ways to motivate teammates and ask your manager if you are being too protective of them.
translations.同事與老闆都是我們很常低估的強大資源，我們可以快速的從同事與老闆的 Feedba: Colleagues and bosses are powerful resources we often underestimate. We can quickly learn from their feedback. Establishing good records and regularly reviewing decisions helps us receive better feedback.
translations.同步的溝通：例如產品開發流程，需要設計資源；在進到工程開發階段，我們是否已經有明: Synchronous Communication: For example, in the product development process, design resources are needed; when entering the engineering development phase, do we already have clear specifications to proceed with development? Or are we still waiting? Is there anything we can do in the meantime?
translations.善用同儕壓力\n將任務拆給隊友共同協作，彼此共同努力(Code Review 亦:
Leverage Peer Pressure  
Break tasks into parts for teammates to collaborate on and work together (Code Review is also part of this).
translations.回頭來看，作者說與其害怕傷害隊友的感覺不如說出內心真實所想的，「害怕傷害隊友」這: Looking back, the author says that instead of fearing hurting teammates' feelings, it's better to speak your true thoughts. The "fear of hurting teammates" is simply a selfish imagination and is unnecessary. Moreover, it is the manager's responsibility to lead the team forward and grow; you must keep the big picture in mind and control risks.
translations.因為商業策略隨時在改變，我們應該對於優化策略保有更開放彈性的想法，優化的總結還是: Because business strategies change constantly, we should maintain an open and flexible mindset toward optimization strategies. Ultimately, optimization should be driven by business needs.
translations.因為已經不像以前純當工程師(Maker)的時候可以全心全力灌注在開發需求上，所以: Since you can no longer fully focus on development tasks as when you were purely an engineer (Maker), you need to choose tasks you can personally execute based on the time available in the engineer's calendar.
translations.團隊與團隊兼協作的默契。\n\n: The rapport between teams and cross-team collaboration.
translations.團隊要有願景，而不是只把任務處理好\n: The team should have a vision, not just focus on completing tasks.
translations.團體的核心價值觀大於個體\n可能導致有人離職，也需要時間耐心才可能實現；也有許多:
The core values of the group outweigh those of individuals.  
This may lead to some people leaving and requires patience and time to achieve; there are also many challenges (e.g., when someone leaves, the core values may be questioned).
translations.在程式中隱藏招募彩蛋(EX: 網頁開發者工具)\n: Hiding Recruitment Easter Eggs in Code (e.g., Web Developer Tools)
translations.培養良好的工程文化環境 (EX: Code Review、年會…)\n: Foster a positive engineering culture environment (e.g., Code Review, annual meetings…)
translations.基礎設施：讓程式碼好擴充、好維護\n: Infrastructure: Making Code Scalable and Maintainable
translations.如何以風險較低的方式完成任務。\n\n: How to Complete Tasks with Lower Risk.
translations.如果不是緊急問題，可以先請隊友留言或彙整資訊寄信給你，等到專心時間結束後再來處理: If it's not an urgent issue, ask your teammates to leave a message or compile the information in an email for you. Then handle it after your focused time is over.
translations.如果你想要有所成就，就必須習慣不會每個人都喜歡你的想法\n\n: If you want to achieve something, you must get used to the fact that not everyone will like your ideas.
translations.委派任務的同時又不喪失品質跟能見度。\n\n: Delegate tasks without losing quality and visibility.
translations.完成好任務甚至優雅地解決難題是優秀的工程師的衡量標準，但做為管理者以不是用完成任: Completing tasks well and elegantly solving problems are measures of a great engineer, but as a manager, success is not measured by task completion ability—that has already been proven. Instead, it is judged by the ability to lead, drive, and elevate the team's goals.
translations.定期 Review 你身為管理者下的決策。\n\n: Regularly review the decisions you make as a manager.
translations.定期與隊友 1:1，互相 Feedback 並分享所學到的東西。\n\n: Regularly have 1:1 meetings with teammates to give mutual feedback and share what you've learned.
translations.將任務拆解成小任務，降低 Deadline 風險\n一開始可能很難，但可以訓練學:
Break down tasks into smaller tasks to reduce deadline risks  
It may be difficult at first, but it can be trained and learned
translations.對團隊所有成員保持忠誠尊重\n有了核心價值在招募或開除更有評斷準則，還有更能有做:
Be Loyal and Respectful to All Team Members  
Having core values provides clear criteria for recruitment or termination and sets a standard for how to get things done.
translations.對於是否要將任務委派給隊友，作者會先問這個任務是否真的只有我能做且是屬於管理者該: When deciding whether to delegate a task to a teammate, the author first asks if the task truly can only be done by themselves and if it belongs to the manager's responsibilities. The second question is whether the task is a long-term leadership task. If neither applies, the task should be delegated to a teammate.
translations.對於要委派的任務可評估隊友的經驗、技能，找到合適的人選。\n\n: For tasks to be delegated, assess teammates' experience and skills to find the right person.
translations.對齊核心價值\n算是共識及默契，對於做事方式、什麼重要的默契；團隊核心價值也不是:
Align Core Values  
This refers to consensus and tacit understanding regarding ways of working and what is important. Team core values are not fixed and should evolve over time.
translations.對齊目標\n對齊個人願景與公司目標，如果不了解當前公司的目標很可能造成團隊功能失:
Aligning Goals  
Align personal vision with company goals. Not understanding the current company goals can lead to team dysfunction.
translations.小組越多就很難統一所有人的價值觀、期望、優先權、隱含的期望。\n\n: The more teams there are, the harder it is to align everyone's values, expectations, priorities, and implicit assumptions.
translations.平時就要開始做以下事項，防止突然缺人才要開始，那只能回到傳統找人方式，不停的面試: You should start doing the following regularly to avoid suddenly facing a talent shortage. Otherwise, you'll have to rely on traditional hiring methods, conducting endless interviews but struggling to find the right candidates.
translations.平衡\n對於團成員的職能、成長，分配不同的願景、自主權、擁有全；互相協作一起成長:
Balance  
Assign different visions, autonomy, and ownership according to team members' skills and growth; collaborate and grow together (e.g., newcomers expect to understand company workflows, veterans handle code reviews and mentoring); everyone should have opportunities for growth.
translations.建立 Side Project (EX: 開源專案)\n: Create Side Projects (EX: Open Source Projects)
translations.建立可擴充程式以是我們之前擔任工程是該有的職責，但現在要挑戰的是打造可擴充團隊。: Building scalable software used to be our responsibility as engineers, but now the challenge is to build scalable teams.
translations.建立商業學習(價值)文化大與建造文化（重點不是建造解法，而是我們試圖解決的商業問: Build a culture of business learning (value) rather than just building solutions (the focus is on the business problems we aim to solve)
translations.建立商業學習文化而不是建造文化、優化吞吐量、優化的價值。\n\n: Building a culture of business learning instead of just building culture, optimizing throughput, and the value of optimization.
translations.引起多少技術問題？\n: How many technical issues does it cause?
translations.忘記統整討論結果（要習慣紀錄 1:1、討論的結果）\n: Forget to consolidate discussion results (Get into the habit of recording 1:1 and discussion outcomes)
translations.感謝 [海總理](https://twitter.com/tzangms){:t: Thanks to [Prime Minister Hai](https://twitter.com/tzangms){:target="_blank"} for the recommendation
translations.應該要關注拆分小組的理由跟動機而非產出，否則可能導致矛盾。\n\n: Focus on the reasons and motivations for splitting the team rather than the output, as otherwise it may lead to conflicts.
translations.成就感\n成果要能有成就感，做為管理者不能讓隊友干燒熱情\n:
## Sense of Achievement
Results should bring a sense of achievement. As a manager, you must not let your team members lose their enthusiasm.
translations.我們不想承認對上或對下一無所知、害怕為團隊成功負責也擔心沒拿捏好技術(債)與商業: We don’t want to admit that we know nothing about our superiors or subordinates, fear taking responsibility for the team’s success, and worry about not balancing technical (debt) and business needs properly.
translations.我們可以列出選項並提供決定給隊友，但與此同時也要記下所做的決定。\n\n: We can list options and provide decisions to teammates, but at the same time, we must also record the decisions made.
translations.我們是團隊，團隊成員有自己的責任跟要交付的成果，同時也要與其他人協作，幫助他人，: We are a team. Each member has their own responsibilities and deliverables, while also collaborating with others, helping each other, and growing together. Defining expectations is like a contract; under the shift from colleague to manager relationships, it enables better and more purposeful leadership. Defining these items is not easy and requires time and patience to iterate.
translations.我們要展現同理心而不是同情心，為了讓他們的工作真正出類拔萃，他們需要我們客觀的意: We need to show empathy, not sympathy. To help them truly excel in their work, they need our objective feedback.
translations.我想也是，如果不是進退兩難的問題應該也不會問你；如果問你就代表隊友不知道該如何決: I think so too. If it weren’t a dilemma, you probably wouldn’t ask me; asking means the teammate doesn’t know how to decide.
translations.我有清楚說明我的期待嗎？\n: Did I clearly state my expectations?
translations.我覺得與我的背景非常相似，都是本來做軟體開發，初探管理職；藉由書中提到的重點讓我: I find it very similar to my background, starting as a software developer and exploring management roles; the key points in the book taught me many practical methods to apply!
translations.所以不是說當管理者就不用做工程師的事，而是兩邊都要碰，需要的就在 工程師(Mak: So being a manager doesn't mean you stop doing engineering work; you need to engage in both and find a balance between the Engineer (Maker) and Manager roles.
translations.持續對內及對外溝通\n對內：確保期望、同步、Deadline、資源，對外：溝通、:
Continuous Internal and External Communication  
Internal: Ensure expectations, alignment, deadlines, and resources.  
External: Communicate clearly, and if time is tight, skip non-essential meetings.
translations.挑選幾個能以身作則的 Task\n: Select a few tasks to lead by example
translations.推動隊友跳脫舒適圈以及不讓自己變成混蛋跟陷入陷阱。\n\n: Encourage teammates to step out of their comfort zones while avoiding becoming a jerk or falling into traps.
translations.提供具有生產力且開心的工作環境\n: Provide a productive and happy work environment
translations.支援、修 Bug、文件\n不是釋出功能就好，還要做好客服支援、修 Bug、還有文:
Support, Bug Fixing, Documentation  
It's not enough to just release features; you also need to provide good customer support, fix bugs, and maintain documentation.
translations.方法有：到會議室、戴上耳機、或甚至買一個 ON AIR ! 的開關燈放桌上。\n: Methods include: going to a meeting room, wearing headphones, or even buying an ON AIR! switch light to place on your desk.
translations.有任何問題及指教歡迎 [與我聯絡](https://www.zhgchg.li/: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to [contact me](https://www.zhgchg.li/contact){:target="_blank"}.
translations.有沒有其他比較有經驗的人能提供建議\n: Is there anyone more experienced who can offer advice?
translations.有沒有問過隊友的建議\n: Have you asked your teammates for their advice?
translations.有能力訓練他人改變\n: Ability to train others to change
translations.為了降低產出的風險，作者建議將需求拆成許多小塊迭代功能；並將此想法與其他 Tea: To reduce the risk of deliverables, the author suggests breaking down requirements into many small iterative features and sharing this idea with other teams.
translations.現在重新思考，還會是同個決定嗎？\n: Would you make the same decision if you reconsider now?
translations.當產品還在草創初試市場狀態時，應該要以優化效率(快速解決任務釋出)為主，重複著以: When the product is still in the early market testing stage, the focus should be on optimizing efficiency (quickly solving tasks and releasing), repeatedly following the process below:
translations.相較於掩蓋，我有公開分享失敗經驗嗎？（可以是寫文章、寄信給所有人）\n: Compared to covering up, have I openly shared my failure experiences? (This can be writing articles or sending emails to everyone)
translations.知道 Task 的 Why 而不是 How\n: Knowing the Why of a Task, Not the How
translations.知道做事的底線 sense\n: Knowing the bottom line sense of getting things done
translations.知道每個優化的 Impact，\n: Know the impact of each optimization,
translations.管理者需要區分出需要與想要\n: Managers Need to Distinguish Between Needs and Wants
translations.管理職，初來乍到，一切都很迷茫；對於管理的知識只有彙整之前的工作經驗、觀察或與其: As a new manager, everything felt confusing; my knowledge of management came only from past work experience, observations, or casual chats with colleagues. I knew which actions by supervisors were seen positively or negatively by their teams, but that was about it. My knowledge was fragmented and lacked a systematic framework. So, I started reading books and taking notes on each author's experiences. When encountering similar situations again, having this "knowledge confidence" helped me stay calm instead of panicking.
translations.簡單的感謝信（感謝協助）\n: Simple Thank You Letter (Thank You for Your Assistance)
translations.給予商業需求可預測性(同上)\n: Provide Predictability for Business Requirements (Same as Above)
translations.自動化的重要（能一勞永逸節省時間）\n: The Importance of Automation (Save Time Once and for All)
translations.自動化：使用自動化處理繁瑣的人工操作，節省時間之餘也能避免出錯\n: Automation: Use automation to handle tedious manual tasks, saving time and reducing errors
translations.與其他團隊對於優先權意見不同時，可解釋引出其他因素（EX: 這個做了之後，可以降: When there is a difference in priority opinions with other teams, explain by bringing up other factors (e.g., completing this will reduce customer complaints, provide a one-time permanent solution, or have a multiplier effect…).
translations.與其他團隊經理建立良好的溝通\n作者建議可以在每兩週的管理者會議分享團隊內的狀態:
Establish Good Communication with Other Team Managers  
The author suggests sharing your team's status, obstacles, challenges, upcoming key tasks, and the reasons behind them during bi-weekly manager meetings.
translations.與社群分享我們團隊遇到的問題及解決方法（文章 or Talk）\n: Share with the community the problems our team encountered and the solutions we implemented (article or talk)
translations.若不可能則要權衡可能會延遲時間或替代方案，並要讓關係人知道（防止在背後指指點點）: If it is not possible, weigh the potential delay or alternative solutions, and inform stakeholders (to prevent gossip behind the scenes).
translations.要做好數據追蹤，才能了解努力的成效\n: To do effective data tracking, you must understand the impact of your efforts
translations.評估功能到釋出每個階段可以優化的地方（花太多時間在設計？在討論？）\n\n: Evaluate areas for improvement at each stage from feature assessment to release (spending too much time on design? on discussions?).
translations.詢問隊友學到的東西、能讓他更積極的動機、討厭的事\n: Ask teammates what they have learned, what motivates them to be more proactive, and what they dislike.
translations.身為工程師時我們有很多方法或工具，只要遵循就能提升好能力，諸如 pair pro: As engineers, we have many methods or tools that can improve our skills if followed, such as pair programming, code review, and design patterns. But as managers, especially beginners, we often feel quite lonely.
translations.身為工程管理者，我們要做的不是埋頭寫 Code 或是全心投入在技術；時不時應該要: As engineering managers, our job is not to bury ourselves in coding or focus solely on technology; we should periodically realign with product value.
translations.身為管理者必須做好任務委派，作者認為委派就該設定好期待並相信被指派的隊友有能力執: As a manager, task delegation must be done well. The author believes delegation should involve setting clear expectations and trusting that the assigned team members are capable, have opportunities to learn, and room to make mistakes. On the other hand, managers also need to protect their team members from company pressure.
translations.身為管理者我們必須展現決策背後的動機\n: As managers, we must demonstrate the motivation behind our decisions
translations.追蹤團隊產出 (EX: 「01/01/2013–14/01/2013: 2 La: Tracking team output (e.g., "01/01/2013–14/01/2013: 2 Large features, 5 Medium features, 4 Small"), based on long-term statistics; this can be used for forecasting.
translations.這五項息息相關，如果因為 Retention 低，可能可以同步調整 Referr: These five factors are closely related. If Retention is low, Referrer and Acquisition can be adjusted simultaneously.
translations.這問題在其他團隊或會重複出現嗎？\n: Does this issue recur in other teams?
translations.這問題真的需要管理者來決定嗎\n: Does this issue really need a manager to decide?
translations.這邊主要紀錄的是對團隊目標重要的任務，日常工作不用紀錄。\n\n: This section mainly records tasks important to the team's goals; routine work does not need to be documented.
translations.過了草創試驗期，產品模式趨於穩定；這時候比較適合優化的是吞吐量(EX: 給定 X: After the initial trial phase, the product model tends to stabilize; at this point, it is more suitable to optimize throughput (e.g., given X resources, achieve Y output).
translations.邀請一位其他 Team 的隊友加入我們的工作\n: Invite a teammate from another team to join our work
translations.關係信任 — — 人們是否以建立和保護關係的方式行事\n: Relationship Trust — Do people act in ways that build and protect relationships?
translations.雖然相較「這功能不做，公司會倒閉」跟「這功能會導致技術債」，前者當然更可怕；對於: Although "the company will fail without this feature" is obviously scarier than "this feature will cause technical debt," as managers, we should strive to secure more time to address technical debt when possible. We must communicate and manage it properly.
translations.養成紀錄的習慣，並且要確保內容是之後能回憶的。\n\n: Develop the habit of documenting, and ensure the content can be recalled later.
